revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Jugoroana]][[en:Jūgorō Yokoanabos]][[ja:十五郎穴横穴群]] [[co:commons:Category:Jugoroana]][[en:Jūgorō Yokoanabos]][[ja:十五郎穴横穴群]] [[co:commons:Category:Jugoroana]][[en:Jūgorō Yokoanabos]][[ja:十五郎穴横穴群]] [[en:Jūgorō Yokoanabos]][[ja:十五郎穴横穴群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Jugoroana, gaikan-2.jpg|location={{ill|Nakane|qid=Q97160643|1=Nakane}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.37141|N|140.570154|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 十五郎穴横穴群 |image =[[File:Jugoroana, gaikan-2.jpg|280px]]
館出支群I区 |所属 = 十五郎穴横穴墓群 |location = [[:en:茨城県|茨城県]][[:en:ひたちなか市|ひたちなか市]]中根3490-イ | coordinates = 36 | coordinates = 22 | coordinates = 17.076 | coordinates = 140 | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 12.554 |アイコン = 横穴墓 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図 = Japan Ibaraki |type = [[横穴墓]] |built = [[:en:古墳時代|古墳時代]]終末期～[[奈良時代]] |Burial Facility = |area =（約300基の横穴墓群） |Interred Persons = |burial goods = 方頭{{ill|大刀|en|大刀|qid=Q1224104|lt=大刀|1=大刀}}、[[蕨手刀]]、[[須恵器]]、[[勾玉]]など |cultural property = 国の[[史跡]]「十五郎穴横穴群」 |Special Notes = 周辺に[[虎塚古墳]]群及び、笠谷古墳群 }} '''Jugoroana Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (十五郎穴横穴群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Nakane, Hitachinaka City, Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}} of Japan[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Press Release, October 20, 2023]Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Announcement No. 12, February 21, 2024., and some burial goods from the site have been Historic site as Historic site of Hitachinaka City. == Overview == The Jugoroana Tomb Group consists of rock-cut tombs dating from the [[Late Kofun tumuli|Final Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. The tombs are distributed across three subgroups: the {{ill|Sashibu Subgroup|ja|指渋支群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashibu Subgroup|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where {{ill|Torazuka Kofun|en|Torazuka Kofun|qid=Q11625282|lt=Torazuka Kofun|1=Torazuka Kofun}} is located; the {{ill|Tatedashi Subgroup|ja|館出支群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatedashi Subgroup|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where Torazuka Kofun No. 2 is found; and the {{ill|Kasaya Subgroup|ja|笠谷支群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaya Subgroup|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which contains the {{ill|Kasaya Kofun Group|ja|笠谷古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaya Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. By 2014, 272 tombs had been confirmed through excavations, and the total number of tombs is estimated to exceed 300. In {{ill|1940|en|1940|qid=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}} (Shōwa 15), a section of the Tatedashi Subgroup (34 tombs) was Historic site as a Historic site of Ibaraki Prefecture. The name "Jugoroana" is said to originate from a legend about the {{ill|Revenge of the Soga Brothers|en|Revenge of the Soga Brothers|qid=Q10324810|lt=Revenge of the Soga Brothers|1=Revenge of the Soga Brothers}}, where {{ill|Soga Tokimune|en|Soga Tokimune|qid=Q11515401|lt=Gorō|1=Soga Tokimune}} and {{ill|Soga Sukenari|en|Soga Sukenari|qid=Q11515406|lt=Jūrō|1=Soga Sukenari}} allegedly hid in the caves. == burial goods == * In {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950|1=1950}}, a {{ill|Sword|en|Sword|qid=Q12791|lt=Sword|1=Sword}} with a square-headed bronze fitting was excavated from Tatedashi Subgroup Tomb No. 32. It was Historic site as a Hitachinaka City designation1 (archaeological material) on [[March 5, 2002]] (Heisei 14). * During the 1976-1980 excavation of the Sashibu Subgroup, 119 tombs were identified. Human remains, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, [[magatama]], and [[kirikodama]] (cut glass beads) were among the grave goods unearthed. * In {{ill|2011|en|2011|qid=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}, an excavation of Tatedashi Subgroup Tomb No. 35 uncovered a dagger ([[tōsu]]) with decorative fittings, including a scabbard-end fitting and belt fittings, along with a [[warabitōtō]] (a curved sword). The dagger exhibits similarities to items stored in the {{ill|Shōsōin|en|Shōsōin|qid=Q2379007|lt=Shōsōin|1=Shōsōin}}, and the warabitōtō represents only the second discovery of its kind in Ibaraki Prefecture. == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Jugoroana Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' - Designated on February 21, 2024. Before its Historic site as a National Historic site, the tomb group was Historic site a Historic site of Ibaraki Prefecture on March 11, 1940. === Hitachinaka City Historic site === * '''Tangible designation1''' ** Bronze-fitting sword excavated from Tatedashi Subgroup Tomb No. 32 - Preserved at the Hitachinaka City Archaeological Research Center. Designated on March 5, 2002. ** Collection of 266 burial goods from Tatedashi Subgroup Tomb No. 35 - Preserved at the Hitachinaka City Archaeological Research Center. Designated on October 16, 2017. == Gallery == === Tatedashi Subgroup I Area === File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb1-13 Number.jpg|Tombs 1-13 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb1-14 Number.jpg|Tomb 1-14 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb11-15 Number.jpg|Tombs 11-15 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb18-21 Number.jpg|Tombs 18-21 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb32-35.jpg|Tombs 32-35 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb1.jpg|Tomb 1 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb2.jpg|Tomb 2 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb3.jpg|Tomb 3 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb4.jpg|Tomb 4 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb5.jpg|Tomb 5 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb7.jpg|Tomb 7 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb9.jpg|Tomb 9 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb13.jpg|Tomb 13 File:Jugoroana Tatedashi-shigun1, Tomb14.jpg|Tomb 14 == References == {{reflist|refs= {{Cite web|typeat=PDF|url=http://www.hitachinaka-sa.com/pdf/shiseki03.pdf|title=Jugoroana Yokoanabo Tomb Group|publisher=Hitachinaka City Tourism Association|accessdate=2015-09-03}} {{Cite web|date=2014-4-5|url=http://www.city.hitachinaka.ibaraki.jp/soshiki/56/bunkazai-syoukai.html|title=Introduction to Hitachinaka City Historic site|publisher=Hitachinaka City|accessdate=2015-09-03}} {{Cite web|date=2014-4-5|url=http://www.city.hitachinaka.ibaraki.jp/soshiki/56/bunkazaiichiran.html|title=List of Hitachinaka City Historic site|publisher=Hitachinaka City|accessdate=2015-09-03}} {{Cite web|url=http://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/ken/shiseki/12-20/12-20.html|title=Jugoroana|publisher=Ibaraki Prefecture Board of Education|accessdate=2015-09-03}} {{Cite web|date=2013-11-26|url=http://www.city.hitachinaka.ibaraki.jp/soshiki/56/tousu-warabite.html|title=Dagger and Warabitōtō Found at Jugoroana Yokoanabo Tomb Group|publisher=Hitachinaka City|accessdate=2015-09-03}} }} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Jugoroana}} * {{ill|Torazuka Kofun|en|Torazuka Kofun|qid=Q11625282|lt=Torazuka Kofun|1=Torazuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Rock-cut tomb|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rock-cut tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} == Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Tombs == === Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgorō-ana Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karanogosho Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoanabo Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hundred Caves|qq=}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi, Saitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūrō Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatoyama, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa|qq=}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) - Metropolitan Historic site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}) - Municipal Historic site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs === Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoanabos|en|Kashiya Yokoanabos|qid=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Yokoanabos|1=Kashiya Yokoanabos}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic site, 92 tombs === Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|en|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|qid=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Yokoanabos|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, over 150 tombs === Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoanabos|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs === Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]]) === Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|qid=Q11419962|lt=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|1=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013).
